input.prompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

<current_datetime>2026-04-04T00:38:13.378Z</current_datetime>

I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page

input.initialPrompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common gotcha when moving from ASP.NET Core to .NET MAUI regarding dependency injection scoping — specifically why `AddScoped` behaves differently in MAUI than in ASP.NET Core.

This is a knowledge question, not a code task. I can answer directly.

In ASP.NET Core, a "scope" is created per HTTP request, so `AddScoped` gives you a new instance per request. In MAUI, there's no concept of an HTTP request scope. By default, MAUI's DI container treats `AddScoped` essentially the same as `AddSingleton` — the scope is the entire application lifetime. This means your DbContext is being reused across all page navigations, which is why data seems to persist (the same DbContext instance with its change tracker is shared everywhere).


The workaround is to either switch to `AddTransient` for a fresh instance each time, or manually manage scopes using `IServiceScopeFactory` when you need more control over the lifetime.
